title: Ww1 vs Ww2
shortAnswer: WW2 was significantly more deadly than WW1, with 70-85 million deaths vs ~20 million. WW2 had far greater civilian casualties and expanded to the Pacific Theatre.

keyDifferences.label: Geographic Scope
keyDifferences.entityBValue: Global war including Pacific Theatre and China

prosA: Lower overall death toll with ~20 million casualties
Fewer civilian casualties (35-40% of total deaths)
Limited geographic scope reduced global devastation
No systematic genocide or Holocaust equivalent
consA: Contributed to conditions that led to WW2
Still caused massive loss of life and destruction
Set precedent for modern industrial warfare
prosB: Led to technological and medical advances
Resulted in establishment of international organizations
consB: Deadliest event in human history with 70-85 million deaths
Massive civilian casualties including deliberate targeting
Introduced unprecedented destruction through strategic bombing
verdict: While both wars were devastating, WW2 was significantly more deadly and destructive than WW1. WW2's expanded geographic scope, systematic targeting of civilians, and advanced warfare technology resulted in 3-4 times more casualties than WW1.
